velocity_vs_rigour_quote: Implement quality assurance protocols for financial data
velocity_vs_rigour_reasoning: Multiple emphasis on correctness, quality, data accuracy, and verification with no velocity signals present.
domain_risk_reasoning: Revenue recognition is core to financial reporting with regulatory/compliance implications; fintech context with explicit financial metrics responsibility.
data_team_maturity_quote: Develop and sustain dimensional models using SQLMesh for revenue recognition and subscription metrics
data_team_maturity_reasoning: The use of 'sustain' indicates existing infrastructure while 'develop' shows ongoing growth, suggesting a team with established tooling building out new capabilities.
jd_authorship_quote: Develop and sustain dimensional models using SQLMesh for revenue recognition and subscription metrics
jd_authorship_reasoning: The responsibility names a specific tool (SQLMesh) with precise business context (revenue recognition, subscription metrics) that only someone doing this job would articulate.
stakeholder_orientation_quote: Collaborate with Finance to convert requirements into structured data solutions
stakeholder_orientation_reasoning: Finance is explicitly named as the primary stakeholder, and the role's responsibilities center entirely on delivering financial metrics and revenue data.
autonomy_level_quote: Collaborate with Finance to convert requirements into structured data solutions
autonomy_level_reasoning: Finance sets the requirements (revenue recognition, churn metrics) and this role executes the technical solution to deliver against those requirements.

ai_role_reasoning: SQLMesh is a data orchestration tool; no mention of AI infrastructure, GenAI, or AI-assisted coding.

testing_framing_reasoning: Verb 'implement' with 'quality assurance protocols' establishes ownership of quality practice.
